name: anthropic

settings_files._hook_note: Usually blocked by Self-Modification hook — expect to fall back to manual paste.
Partial block — agent edits allowed for env block and new top-level keys. DO NOT anchor edits near skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt / skipAutoPermissionPrompt.

persistent_files.note: Plaintext session transcripts, 30-day default retention. Tune with top-level cleanupPeriodDays in settings.json.

provider_switches.note: Routes to AWS Bedrock; telemetry off by default there
Routes to Google Vertex AI; telemetry off by default there
Routes to Azure AI Foundry; telemetry off by default there

notes: CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC is the kill switch but does NOT cover skipWebFetchPreflight.
Prompts/outputs still go to Anthropic's API unless a provider switch is used (changes billing/compute, not just telemetry).

TRADE-OFF (surface to the user before applying): CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC and DISABLE_TELEMETRY share the same GrowthBook kill-switch with FEATURE ENTITLEMENTS — not just telemetry. Any Claude Code capability gated by a remote feature flag silently disappears when these are set. Confirmed victim: Opus 4.6 1M-context default model on Max/Team/Enterprise plans (documented upstream). Reported victim: /remote-control slash command vanishes after these vars are set (user-observed 2026-04-24, not yet reproduced via isolated `env -u` session). GrowthBook-gated features may fail similarly in the future. Upstream: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/34178. If the user needs a feature that disappears, narrow the opt-out — drop these two vars and keep DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING, DISABLE_FEEDBACK_COMMAND, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY (not known / documented to touch GrowthBook).
